data.title: BlackRock's iShares Highlights Ethereum Spot ETF on Homepage
data.description: BlackRock's iShares has prominently featured its Ethereum spot ETF, ETHB, supported by staking, at the top of its homepage, which already includes around 500 ETFs listed on U.S. stock exchanges. According to NS3.AI, BlackRock filed an amended S-1 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) last month for this product. Nate Geraci noted that the placement is significant considering the extensive range of existing iShares offerings.

data.title: Hyperliquid Sees Record Trading Volume and Open Interest
data.description: Official data reveals that over the past two weeks, Hyperliquid's open interest in real-world assets (RWA) has exceeded $1.3 billion. According to Foresight News, weekend trading volume reached $1.4 billion, marking a new all-time high.

data.title: Bank of England Rate Hike Probability at 54% by 2026 End
data.description: Interest rate futures indicate a 54% probability that the Bank of England will raise interest rates by 25 basis points by the end of 2026. According to Jin10, this forecast reflects market expectations regarding monetary policy adjustments in response to economic conditions. The potential rate hike is being closely monitored by investors and analysts as they assess its implications for the UK economy and financial markets.

data.title: South Korea Develops System to Track Cryptocurrency Investment Gains
data.description:
South Korea's National Tax Service (NTS) announced on March 12 that it is developing a system to track cryptocurrency investment gains. According to BlockBeats, this initiative aligns with the government's expansive fiscal policy and the need to increase revenue. The system is being constructed ahead of the government's plan to tax virtual asset profits starting January next year.

The NTS has issued a tender for the 'Virtual Asset Transaction Analysis Integrated System' project, with the procurement office responsible for government and public institution purchases posting it on its electronic bidding platform. The project is valued at 3 billion Korean won (approximately $2.02 million).

The selected bidder will be chosen and contracted within this month, with system design commencing in April. After multiple rounds of testing, the system is expected to enter trial operation in November and officially launch within the year.

The NTS stated that the system will begin collecting individual virtual asset transaction data from 2027, aiming to systematically manage and analyze vast amounts of transaction information to more effectively identify tax evasion, including uncovering hidden income through tax audits.

Notably, the NTS plans to incorporate artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies to analyze and track abnormal transaction types and patterns. Additionally, relevant virtual asset analysis data and lists of suspects will be shared with other government departments, including the Korea Customs Service, Statistics Korea, and the Bank of Korea.

Under South Korean tax law, starting January next year, virtual asset annual earnings exceeding 2.5 million Korean won will be subject to a 22% comprehensive tax rate, which includes a 20% income tax and a 2% local income tax.

data.title: Amazon Plans Major Investment in Poland by 2028
data.description: Amazon has announced plans to invest 23 billion Polish złoty in Poland between 2026 and 2028. According to Jin10, this significant investment is part of Amazon's strategy to expand its operations and infrastructure in the region. The move is expected to enhance Amazon's presence in the European market, potentially boosting local economic growth and job creation. The investment underscores Amazon's commitment to strengthening its global footprint and supporting regional development initiatives.

data.title: Oil Prices Narrow as U.S. Plans Strategic Reserve Release Amid Iran Conflict
data.description:
Oil prices for WTI and Brent crude have narrowed, with WTI briefly dropping below $90, according to BlockBeats. Hyperinsight monitoring indicates a 4.2% decline from the day's peak at noon, currently priced at $90.1.

In response to the conflict in Iran, U.S. President Donald Trump's administration plans to release 172 million barrels of oil from the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve. This move is part of a broader initiative by International Energy Agency (IEA) member countries to release a total of 400 million barrels globally. The announcement, made at noon, prompted a swift market reaction, leading to a pullback in pre-market oil gains.

data.title: RBC Predicts Fed to Hold Rates Steady Amid Rising Risks
data.description: The Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) has indicated that the risks to the Federal Reserve's dual mandate of achieving maximum employment and maintaining price stability have increased. According to Jin10, despite these rising risks, RBC still anticipates that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates unchanged in 2026. The bank's report suggests that recent energy price shocks are not sufficient to prompt rate hikes but may lead the Fed to adopt a wait-and-see approach. RBC Capital Markets expresses growing concern over the downside risks to economic growth if energy price shocks persist. The bank believes the Federal Reserve shares this perspective. Looking ahead to 2027, RBC forecasts three 25-basis-point "normalization" rate cuts as inflation more sustainably returns to target levels.

data.title: Israeli Defense Forces Eliminate Hezbollah Missile Unit Commander
data.description: The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) have announced the elimination of Abu Zahr Mohammadi, the operational commander of the missile unit within Hezbollah, which is linked to the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps. According to Jin10, this development marks a significant move in the ongoing regional tensions involving Israel, Hezbollah, and Iran. The IDF's action is part of broader efforts to counter threats posed by Hezbollah's military capabilities, particularly its missile arsenal. The situation remains tense as both sides continue to engage in strategic maneuvers.

data.title: Plume's RWA Value Grows Despite Significant Decline from Peak
data.description: Plume has experienced a notable increase in its Real-World Asset (RWA) value over the past 30 days, with a growth of 44.19%. According to NS3.AI, despite this positive trend, PLUME remains down more than 90% from its peak last year. The network currently holds approximately $299 million across 35 assets. Additionally, exchange balances have risen above 1.57 billion PLUME, accounting for 15.67% of the total supply.

data.title: The Wealth of Nations: 250 Years of Influence and Misinterpretation
data.description: Adam Smith's seminal work, The Wealth of Nations, marks its 250th anniversary this week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting its enduring relevance and the frequent misunderstandings surrounding its insights. The book continues to offer enlightening perspectives on economic principles, shaping discussions and policies even today. Despite its age, The Wealth of Nations remains a cornerstone in the study of economics, influencing thinkers and policymakers across the globe.

data.title: Grvt releases complete token economic model; TGE date adjusted to end of June.
data.description: Decentralized contract exchange Grvt has released the complete economic model for its token GRVT. The total supply of GRVT is 1 billion, distributed as follows: 1. Investors: 19.9%; 2. Team/Core Contributors: 19%; 3. Community/Airdrop: 28%; 4. Future Distribution/Rewards: 33.1%. Furthermore, the TGE confirmation time for GRVT has been adjusted to the end of June 2026.

data.title: Renewable Energy Projects in Vietnam Affected by Retrospective Tariff Adjustments
data.description: Renewable energy projects in Vietnam are facing challenges due to retrospective tariff adjustments, impacting a total capacity of 12 gigawatts. According to Jin10, chambers of commerce from the European Union, the United Kingdom, Japan, and South Korea have expressed concerns over these adjustments. The affected projects are significant for the renewable energy sector, as they represent a substantial portion of Vietnam's energy capacity. The chambers of commerce are urging for a resolution to ensure the continued growth and stability of renewable energy investments in the region.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Israeli Startup Secures $150 Million for AI Customer Service Expansion
data.description: Israeli startup Wonderful has successfully raised $150 million to advance its AI-driven customer service agents. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's plans to utilize the funds for expanding its AI capabilities and enhancing customer interactions. The investment marks a significant milestone for Wonderful, as it aims to revolutionize customer service through artificial intelligence. The startup's innovative approach is expected to streamline operations and improve customer satisfaction across various sectors. This funding round underscores the growing interest in AI technologies and their potential to transform traditional business practices.

data.title: Iran Faces Intensifying Water Crisis Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iran is experiencing an escalating water crisis exacerbated by ongoing conflict, climate change, and years of governmental mismanagement. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the war has intensified the country's already severe water scarcity issues. The combination of environmental challenges and administrative failures has left Iran struggling to manage its water resources effectively. This situation poses significant risks to the nation's agriculture, economy, and overall stability, as authorities grapple with finding sustainable solutions to address the growing crisis.

data.title: Pump.fun becomes Solana's first platform to reach $1 billion in cumulative revenue.
data.description: Since its launch in early 2024, Pump.fun has accumulated approximately $1.08 billion in revenue, becoming the first platform on the Solana blockchain to surpass $1 billion in revenue. According to DefiLlama data, the platform's revenue was $321.3 million in 2024, $664 million in 2025, and $98.3 million so far in 2026. In comparison, Jupiter, a decentralized exchange aggregator in the Solana ecosystem, has accumulated $401.3 million in revenue since its launch, and Raydium, an automated market maker, has accumulated $126.9 million. Pump.fun uses almost all of its protocol revenue for PUMP token buybacks. On March 11, the platform executed a buyback of approximately $1.25 million, representing 99.93% of the previous day's revenue. Since the start of the buyback program, the platform has cumulatively bought back approximately $323.4 million worth of PUMP tokens, representing 28.8% of the circulating supply. Despite this, PUMP is currently trading below its initial offering price of $0.004 and is still significantly lower than its all-time high of $0.0088. Furthermore, public domain records show that Pump.fun has created subdomains pointing to Ethereum, Base, BSC, and Monad, and the platform has also removed the Solana geotag from its X account. Previously, Pump.fun added support for tokens created on platforms such as Raydium and Meteora to its mobile app and acquired the cross-chain trading terminal Vyper.

data.title: Eli Lilly Issues Warning on Zepbound's Interaction with Vitamin B12
data.description: Eli Lilly has issued a warning regarding the potential risks associated with the active ingredient in Zepbound when combined with vitamin B12. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that this interaction could pose health risks to patients. The pharmaceutical company advises healthcare providers and patients to be cautious and consult medical professionals before using these substances together. Eli Lilly's announcement underscores the importance of monitoring drug interactions to ensure patient safety. The company is committed to providing updates as more information becomes available.

data.title: Bitget Wallet integrates with the Stellar mainnet, expanding stablecoin payment infrastructure.
data.description: Bitget Wallet has announced a strategic partnership with decentralized payment network Stellar and integrated its mainnet with Stellar to accelerate the everyday use of stablecoins in cross-border and consumer scenarios. This collaboration will be implemented in phases. Currently, Bitget Wallet has integrated the Stellar mainnet into its payment products, including QR code payments, bank transfers, and deposits/withdrawals. Users can make USDC payments and settlements through the Stellar network and hold, exchange, and transfer Stellar network assets such as XLM, USDC, and PYUSD within the app. Bitget Wallet will also introduce an automated XLM distribution tool to improve user activation efficiency and lower the barrier to first-time use. Stellar is a blockchain network designed for fast, low-cost cross-border payments and transfers, and several USD-compliant stablecoins have chosen to be issued on Stellar. Currently, Stellar has partnered with payment giants such as Stripe, MoneyGram, U.S. Bancorp, Société Générale, Franklin Templeton, WisdomTree, PwC, Circle, and PayPal, and is deeply involved in discussions on U.S. stablecoin policies.

data.title: Private Credit Market Faces Investor Exodus
data.description:
The $1.8 trillion private credit market is experiencing a significant departure of investors. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing concerns among market participants. This shift is attributed to rising interest rates and economic uncertainties, prompting investors to reassess their strategies.

The private credit sector, which has been a popular alternative to traditional lending, is now facing challenges as investors seek safer options. The market's rapid growth in recent years has been fueled by its appeal as a source of higher yields compared to conventional bonds.

However, the current economic climate has led to increased caution among investors, who are now prioritizing stability over potential returns. This trend is impacting the availability of capital for private credit funds, which rely heavily on investor contributions.

As the market adjusts to these changes, industry experts are closely monitoring the situation to gauge its long-term implications. The shift in investor sentiment underscores the need for private credit funds to adapt to evolving market conditions and reassess their risk management strategies.

data.title: Utah Moves to Ban Prediction Market Platforms Amid Regulatory Dispute
data.description:
The state of Utah is poised to prohibit prediction market platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, intensifying a conflict between state regulators and federal authorities over industry regulation. According to Cointelegraph, the Utah House passed the HB243 (Gambling Revisions) bill on February 10, and it cleared the Senate on February 27, now awaiting the governor's approval. The bill categorizes 'proposition betting' as gambling, targeting wagers on individual events within a game rather than the game's final outcome. The legislation aims to prevent companies from offering sports-related prediction or prop betting in Utah, even if they present themselves as prediction markets instead of sportsbooks.

Governor Spencer Cox has expressed his intention to sign the bill, as reported by the Associated Press. He criticized the platforms, stating, 'We are putting a casino in the pocket of every single American, and they are targeting especially young people. It is really awful what they are doing, and we are going to make sure this doesn’t happen in our state.'

In response, Kalshi filed a lawsuit against Utah in February, seeking a federal court's intervention to prevent the state from enforcing its gambling restrictions on the platform. Kalshi contends that its event contracts are federally regulated derivatives, not gambling, and argues that the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has exclusive jurisdiction over these markets under the Commodity Exchange Act, thus prohibiting states like Utah from banning them. On Wednesday, Kalshi also initiated legal action against Iowa, citing the risk of imminent enforcement actions. This follows an Ohio federal judge's decision on Monday to deny Kalshi's request to prevent state regulators from applying gambling laws to its sports event contracts.

The CFTC has maintained its stance on having regulatory authority over prediction markets. Chairman Michael Selig recently stated, 'To those who seek to challenge our authority in this space, let me be clear, we will see you in court.' Speaking at an industry conference in Florida, Selig highlighted the potential of prediction markets as tools for forecasting future events, describing them as 'truth machines' that can provide transparent and accountable signals, potentially more reliable than traditional opinion polls.

data.title: Informa Delays Nearly 12 Business Conferences in the Middle East
data.description: Informa, a leading global events company, has postponed nearly 12 business conferences originally scheduled to take place in the Middle East. According to Jin10, this decision reflects the company's strategic adjustments in response to current market conditions. The move highlights the ongoing challenges faced by the events industry in adapting to regional and global uncertainties. Informa's decision underscores the importance of flexibility and responsiveness in the face of evolving circumstances.
